Fresh D-IV graduate in Refinery Instrumentation Engineering from PEM Akamigas (GPA 3.57/4.00), with hands-on field experience across geothermal and gold-mining plants. I'm now looking for work in my field — in oil & gas, geothermal, or mining.

Profile

About

01 / 06

I recently completed my Applied Bachelor (D-IV) in Refinery Instrumentation Engineering at Politeknik Energi dan Mineral Akamigas (PEM Akamigas) in Cepu, specializing in instrumentation, process control, and automation.

What I enjoy most is the hands-on side of the work. Across internships in geothermal power and gold processing, I've calibrated pressure, temperature, level and flow transmitters, tuned PID control loops with live step tests, performed loop checks on 4–20 mA signals, and troubleshot control valves, RTDs and three-phase motors out on the plant floor. I care about getting measurements right, because everything downstream — safety, efficiency, decisions — depends on them.

I'm comfortable moving between the control room and the field: reading P&IDs, working on DCS and SCADA platforms like Yokogawa CENTUM VP, and building IoT monitoring systems from sensor to dashboard. Now I'm looking for a role where I can keep learning and contribute to reliable, well-instrumented operations.

A bit more on how I got here: my interest in instrumentation actually started outside the classroom, tinkering with Arduino and ESP32-based IoT projects — things like remote fuel-level monitoring and water-level control systems. That hobby turned into a habit of breaking things down into sensor, signal, logic, and output, which turned out to map almost directly onto how industrial instrumentation works. By the time I got to my internships, that mental model made it much easier to pick up real plant instruments quickly.

— Muhammad Dzaky Arrasyid

Where I've been on the ground

Experience

02 / 06
Oct 2025 — Jan 2026 Geothermal
PT Geo Dipa Energi — Unit Patuha
Instrumentation & Control Maintenance Intern
Bandung, West Java
  • Calibrated transmitters across multiple well points — differential pressure, level, and pressure transmitters using the Emerson AMS Trex Communicator and a WIKA pressure calibrator, and temperature transmitters and temperature gauges using a thermobath and a Fluke dry-block calibrator.
  • Conducted orifice sizing and pressure-loss analysis for Production Well 01 in accordance with ISO 5167 standards.
  • Troubleshot and replaced RTD temperature sensors at cooling towers — cable routing, sensor replacement, rewiring, and re-testing to restore normal readings.
  • Adjusted flow instruments at PPL-5 & PPL-6 using an Emerson HART Communicator to match operational setpoints.
  • Calibrated pressure switches, pressure gauges, control valves, conductivity sensors, RTD PT100, temperature gauges, and pH sensors.
  • Performed current and voltage checks on transformers using a Fluke 773 Process Clamp Meter (≈ 9.23 A measured).
  • Performed loop tests on a Honeywell Flow Indicator Transmitter to verify 4–20 mA signal integrity (reading 67.50 t/h, confirmed 15.953 mA output).
  • Conducted Megger insulation resistance tests on transformers (HV–Ground, LV–Ground, HV–LV) using a 1000–2500 V insulation tester.
Jul 2025 — Sep 2025 Gold Mining
PT Agincourt Resources
Electrical & Instrumentation Intern
Tapanuli, North Sumatra
  • Measured voltage and current on motor control panels with a Fluke multimeter to verify electrical performance.
  • Calibrated Control Valves (JOFRA CSC 100), Temperature Sensors (RTD & Thermocouple via Ametek JOFRA and Fluke 754), Pressure Transmitters, and Level Transmitters for signal accuracy and reliability.
  • Troubleshot Control Valves (YT-3300), Flow Transmitters, and Variable Speed Drives (VSDs), focusing on signal verification, actuator performance, and wiring inspection.
  • Installed and aligned Proximity Sensors in the Pebble Crusher area to ensure accurate signal transmission.
  • Performed PID control tuning (Step Test Method) on the Cyanide Injection to Copper Elution Pump System to optimize process performance.
  • Adjusted cyanide system pressure to 12 bar via the Yokogawa application to improve injection efficiency.
  • Calibrated pH sensors at Sed Dam 1, Sed Dam 2 Inlet, and Sed Dam 2 Outlet using pH 4, 7, and 10 buffer solutions.
  • Troubleshot safety relays at the MCC to identify and resolve faults in the motor protection system.
  • Performed 3-phase motor current, voltage, and resistance checks in the CIL and Recyn areas using a Fluke multimeter and Megger tester (U, V, W terminals).
Things I've built

Projects

03 / 06
PRJ.01 · Capstone

Design & Build of a Real-Time IoT-Based Monitoring and Data-Logging System for a Prototype Fuel-Filling System

Jan — Jun 2026

Final-year capstone project: a real-time monitoring and data-logging system for a prototype fuel-filling station that tracks tank level, filling rate, and pump status from sensor to dashboard. The control logic runs on an ESP32, including PID-based pump control that keeps the fill rate consistent, with no surge at the start or tail-off at the end. Data flows through Node-RED into a MySQL database; a local Nextion HMI gives on-site operators a touchscreen view, while a web dashboard shows the same data and is accessible remotely (including by other people), so the plant can be monitored as a remote plant. I built it end-to-end myself: wiring, firmware, the Node-RED flows, the database schema, and the dashboard.

ESP32Node-REDMySQLNextion HMIWeb
PRJ.02 · Control

Water-Level Control

Apr 2024

An IoT- and web-based water-level control system built around an ultrasonic distance sensor and an ESP32 microcontroller. The user sets a target level anywhere from 0–100%, and the ESP32 continuously compares the live ultrasonic reading against that setpoint to decide when to open or close the inlet. I exposed the whole thing over a simple web interface, so the setpoint can be changed and the live level monitored remotely, not just from a local panel. This was an earlier project of mine, and it's where I first got comfortable with the idea of a feedback loop (measure, compare to setpoint, act, and measure again), which turned out to be the exact same logic I later saw at industrial scale during my internships.

ESP32UltrasonicSetpoint 0–100%Web
PRJ.03 · DCS

Tank T-201 Level Control

Nov 2024

A DCS-based fuel-oil level control scheme for Tank T-201, designed and configured on a Yokogawa CENTUM VP system as a campus practicum project. It covered the full configuration path you'd see in a real plant project: drawing the control block diagram, configuring the Human Interface Station (HIS), mapping AI/AO/DI/DO signal points to the appropriate function blocks, and verifying that the operator-workstation interaction behaved correctly under both normal control and simulated upset conditions. Working through this gave me practical familiarity with how a DCS structures a control strategy: not just the PID block itself, but the whole chain of alarms, interlocks, and operator graphics around it.

CENTUM VPDCSHISAI/AO/DI/DO
